title: GLBA §314.4(d)(2) — Continuous monitoring: security-event detection and response-readiness evidence
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=GLBA, clause=§314.4(d)(2), available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting GLBA §314.4(d)(2) (Continuous monitoring). Monitors security-event detection coverage and incident-response readiness; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GLBA §314.4(d)(2) (Continuous monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.39: GLBA §314.4(d)(2) — Continuous monitoring: security-event detection and response-readiness evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.39' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Splunk ES notable events, IDS/IPS alerts, SIEM correlation rules, incident-response ticket logs.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
(index=notable sourcetype=stash) OR (index=incident_response sourcetype=ir_tickets) earliest=-30d
| eval detected=if(sourcetype="stash" AND isnotnull(rule_name), 1, 0)
| eval responded=if(sourcetype="ir_tickets" AND status IN ("acknowledged", "investigating", "resolved"), 1, 0)
| eval response_time_hrs=if(responded=1, round((acknowledged_time - detection_time)/3600, 1), null())
| stats sum(detected) AS detections sum(responded) AS responses avg(response_time_hrs) AS avg_response_hrs dc(rule_name) AS active_rules dc(eval(if(sourcetype="ir_tickets", ticket_id, null()))) AS ir_tickets BY security_domain
| eval detection_to_response_pct=if(detections>0, round(100*responses/detections, 1), 0)
| where detection_to_response_pct<95 OR avg_response_hrs>4
| table security_domain, detections, responses, detection_to_response_pct, avg_response_hrs, active_rules, ir_tickets
description: Monitors security-event detection rates, incident-response coverage, and response times to provide evidence that continuous monitoring per GLBA §314.4(d)(2) is operational.
value: §314.4(d)(2) requires continuous monitoring to detect actual and attempted attacks or intrusions. Tracking detection-to-response ratios and response times demonstrates the monitoring programme is effective.
implementation: (1) Correlate ES notables with incident-response tickets; (2) schedule daily; (3) escalate low detection-to-response ratios and slow response times to SOC management.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: GLBA §314.4(d)(2) requires continuously watching for security threats — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Splunk ES notable events, IDS/IPS alerts, SIEM correlation rules, incident-response ticket logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Correlate ES notables with incident-response tickets; (2) schedule daily; (3) escalate low detection-to-response ratios and slow response times to SOC management.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=notable sourcetype=stash) OR (index=incident_response sourcetype=ir_tickets) earliest=-30d
| eval detected=if(sourcetype="stash" AND isnotnull(rule_name), 1, 0)
| eval responded=if(sourcetype="ir_tickets" AND status IN ("acknowledged", "investigating", "resolved"), 1, 0)
| eval response_time_hrs=if(responded=1, round((acknowledged_time - detection_time)/3600, 1), null())
| stats sum(detected) AS detections sum(responded) AS responses avg(response_time_hrs) AS avg_response_hrs dc(rule_name) AS active_rules dc(eval(if(sourcetype="ir_tickets", ticket_id, null()))) AS ir_tickets BY security_domain
| eval detection_to_response_pct=if(detections>0, round(100*responses/detections, 1), 0)
| where detection_to_response_pct<95 OR avg_response_hrs>4
| table security_domain, detections, responses, detection_to_response_pct, avg_response_hrs, active_rules, ir_tickets
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
